question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted EVM address as the idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter that resolves via EntityService.getEncodedId() to a different account's encoded ID, causing the mirror node to return staking reward history belonging to another account and thereby presenting a falsified view of Hashgraph consensus history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a base32 alias that matches multiple alive entities (duplicate alias DB state) to trigger the multipleAliasMatch error path in EntityService.getAccountFromAlias(), causing the rewards endpoint to crash and permanently suppress the historical staking reward record for that account from being visible?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user submit repeated timestamp=eq:X query parameters to the /rewards endpoint, causing updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to build an IN-clause with attacker-controlled timestamp values that filters out legitimate consensus timestamps, effectively hiding real staking reward history from the response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:0 filter to the /rewards endpoint, causing the query to return all staking reward history from the genesis of the ledger for any account, leaking the complete unfiltered Hashgraph staking history without any authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp=lte:X and timestamp=gte:Y range where X < Y, causing the SQL query built by extractStakingRewardsQuery() to return an empty result set that misrepresents the historical staking reward record as if no rewards were ever paid, constituting a falsified view of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the pagination link returned by utils.getPaginationLink() by replaying a stale next link with a modified timestamp value, causing the server to return a different page of historical staking reward records than intended, breaking the integrity of the paginated history view?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum allowed number of repeated timestamp=eq:X parameters (up to maxRepeatedQueryParameters) to force the IN-clause in updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to include only attacker-chosen consensus timestamps, causing the response to present a cherry-picked subset of staking reward history as if it were the complete record?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted EVM address path parameter that resolves via EntityService.getEncodedId() to a victim account's encoded ID, causing the NFT ownership history returned by NftService.getNfts() to reflect the victim's historical NFT holdings, leaking sensitive Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply token.id=gte:0 without a serialnumber filter to cause the multi-union query to scan the entire NFT history table for an account, returning all historical NFT ownership records and leaking the complete Hashgraph NFT transfer history for that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply conflicting token.id bounds (token.id=gte:X and token.id=lte:Y where X > Y) to cause the multi-union query to return an empty result set, making it appear as though an account has no NFT history when it actually does, constituting a falsified view of Hashgraph NFT ownership history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validateFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply exactly one spender.id=lt:X and one spender.id=gt:Y filter (which passes the multiple-range check) to construct a range query that excludes specific spenders from the NFT history, causing the response to omit historical NFT approvals and misrepresent the on-chain approval history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a limit=1 parameter combined with a crafted token.id lower bound to paginate through NFT history one record at a time, and by replaying pagination links with modified token.id values, reconstruct a falsified ordering of historical NFT ownership that differs from the actual Hashgraph consensus order?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a numeric account ID that corresponds to a deleted entity (where isValidAccount() returns false) to trigger a NotFoundError, and by doing so, determine whether a specific account existed at a given point in Hashgraph history, leaking historical entity existence information?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=eq:X filter where X is a future nanosecond timestamp, causing the staking rewards query to return an empty result set that misrepresents the historical record as having no rewards at that consensus timestamp, when in fact the timestamp has not yet occurred?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=asc combined with a timestamp=gte:X filter to cause the staking rewards history to be returned in ascending order starting from an arbitrary consensus timestamp, allowing the attacker to reconstruct the full chronological staking reward history of any account without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a serialnumber=gte:1 filter without a token.id filter, causing validateSecondaryBound() to throw an InvalidArgumentError that reveals internal filter key names (serial_number, token_id) in the error message, leaking schema information useful for further Hashgraph history manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id=eq:X filter combined with a token.id=eq:Y filter to query the NFT history for a specific token and spender combination, revealing whether a specific approval existed at any point in Hashgraph history for that account, leaking historical approval records?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple spender.id=eq:X filters (which are routed to spenderIdInFilters rather than spenderIdFilters) to bypass the multiple-range validation in validateFilters() and construct an IN-clause with arbitrary spender IDs, causing the NFT history to be filtered to show only NFTs approved to attacker-chosen spenders?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a very large limit value to the /rewards endpoint, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to return the entire staking reward history for an account in a single response, leaking the complete Hashgraph staking history without pagination controls limiting exposure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=lte:X filter where X is a very large nanosecond value (e.g., 9999999999999999999) to cause the query to return all staking reward history up to the maximum possible consensus timestamp, effectively dumping the entire historical staking record for any account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=eq:X filter with a token ID that does not exist, causing NftService.getNfts() to return an empty result set, and by iterating over token IDs, determine which token IDs have historical NFT records for a given account, leaking Hashgraph NFT history metadata?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account alias that resolves to a different account than intended due to alias reuse after account deletion, causing the staking reward history of the new account to be returned instead of the deleted account's history, presenting a falsified view of Hashgraph staking history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:X and timestamp=lte:X (same value) filter combination to query for staking rewards at a specific consensus timestamp, and by iterating over timestamps, reconstruct the exact consensus timestamps of all staking reward events in Hashgraph history for any account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted base32 alias as the idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter that resolves to a victim account, causing the NFT history endpoint to return the victim's complete NFT ownership history, leaking sensitive Hashgraph history without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply token.id=gte:X and serialnumber=gte:Y filters to cause getLowerFilters() to construct a lower-bound subquery that starts from an attacker-chosen position in the NFT history, allowing the attacker to skip over specific historical NFT records and present a truncated view of Hashgraph NFT ownership history?
